requirement: Jurisdictional Registry: Maintain registry of reporting thresholds and timelines (e.g., GDPR 72-hour rule, SEC 4-day rule, AI Act, state breach laws).
Deadline Tracking: Track deadlines and maintain submission evidence for audit (e.g., email receipts, portal screenshots).
Coordinate with Legal, DPO, and Compliance to ensure accuracy and legal sufficiency.
Pre-Approved Reporting Templates: Maintain pre-vetted legal notification templates for common breach scenarios to minimize drafting time during the critical 72-hour window.
Record Retention: Regulatory reports retained per retention policy.
Data Standardization: Use standard internal forms to collect the specific data points (e.g., "number of affected records") required for the external forms.
Legal Approval: Obtain legal counsel approval before releasing information.
Contact Maintenance: Maintain pre-established contact points with law enforcement (FBI, Secret Service) and CERTs.
Evidence Integrity: Preserve digital evidence and maintain strict chain-of-custody documentation.

Privilege & Liability Protocol: Establish a protocol for routing law enforcement communications through legal counsel to maintain attorney-client privilege and maximize potential "safe harbor" protections under laws like CISA's CIRCIA.
Notification Process: Law enforcement notification processes defined with SLA; evidence logs signed and verified.
Scope Verification: Verify scope of engagement and specific data sharing requirements.
Sharing Policy: Develop information-sharing policies covering classification, anonymization, and disclosure approval.
Data Validation: Share only validated and non-sensitive threat data (IOCs, TTPs) to prevent false alarms or privacy leaks.

Technical Sanitization: Implement a mandatory review process (manual or automated) to strip PII, internal hostnames, and proprietary code snippets from threat intelligence artifacts before dissemination.
Tagging Standards: Follow standard data sensitivity tagging (e.g., TLP:RED, TLP:AMBER) for all shared intelligence.
Active Participation: Participation in at least one sector ISAC or trusted community; quarterly sharing activity documented.
Approval Logging: Maintain an approval log of shared information and authorized recipients.
Coordination: Coordinate with Legal, DPO, and Privacy Office to determine notification scope and method.
Notice Content: Prepare plain-language notices disclosing what data was affected, when, and remediation offered.
Templates & Approvals: Document statutory timeframes; maintain notification templates approved by Legal and DPO.

Support Infrastructure Readiness: Scale customer support capacity (call centers, chatbots) and provide agents with specific "Breach FAQ" scripts before notifications are released to handle the influx of inquiries.
Delivery Channels: Define approved verified delivery channels (email, postal, or in-app).
Logging & Tracking: Define process and maintain logs of all notifications and responses.
Coordinate messaging with HR, Legal, and Security to maintain a unified internal narrative.
Standardized Templates: Maintain standardized internal communication templates for incident alerts and updates.

Trusted Channel Verification: Establish and train staff on a primary channel  and a challenge-response protocol to verify that incident instructions are genuine, mitigating social engineering attacks.
Knowledge Repository: Store internal FAQs and quick-reference guides in a centralized repository.
Message Clarity: Verify clarity, consistency, and accessibility of internal messages.
Acknowledgment Tracking: Track message acknowledgment receipts to ensure awareness.
Testing & Validation: Incident communication templates tested during annual tabletop exercises.
Briefing Content: Summarize incident scope, regulatory exposure, and reputational impact.
Decision Logs: Maintain records of decisions, approvals, and strategic directives issued during incidents.
Notification SLA: Major incidents briefed to executive management within 24 hours; executive report retained as evidence.

Materiality Determination: Establish a formal protocol for the Executive Committee to review technical impact data and issue a formal "Materiality" determination to trigger regulatory disclosure workflows.
Standardized Templates: Develop and maintain standardized executive incident report templates.
Risk Metrics: Include metrics linked to organizational risk appetite and materiality thresholds.
Strategic Alignment: Align briefings with continuity, recovery, and stakeholder reporting obligations.
Communication Discipline: Establish protocols for information security—no external disclosures without approval; "need-to-know" enforcement.
Secure Channels: Use secure, dedicated collaboration platforms (e.g., encrypted signal/wire, separate Slack workspace) for IR coordination, distinct from standard corporate comms.
Decision Logs: Maintain chronological decision logs and key communications.
War Room Protocols: Define the virtual or physical "War Room" structure, assigning specific roles (Commander, Scribe, Liaison) to prevent cross-talk and ensure efficient information flow.
Centralized Dashboard: Update all stakeholders via a centralized incident dashboard or status page.
Evidence Integrity: Ensure all updates are timestamped and preserved for after-action review.
Isolation Strategy: Isolated communication channel created per incident; decision log retained with each incident's after-action report.
Authorized Personnel: Only authorized personnel (e.g., C-Suite, PR Head) issue public statements; explicit prohibition on unauthorized staff speaking to media.
Legal & Risk Review: All communications undergo Legal, Risk, and Executive review prior to release to ensure accuracy and limit liability.
Predefined Templates: Use predefined media templates and Q&A documents (holding statements) for various scenarios (e.g., Ransomware, Data Leak, AI Bias Event).

Dissemination Channel Hardening: Enforce phishing-resistant Multi-Factor Authentication (e.g., Hardware Keys) on all social media and PR accounts used to issue public updates to prevent account hijacking and disinformation during the crisis.
AI Transparency: Ensure AI-related disclosures meet jurisdictional expectations (e.g., explicit notice if an AI system was the vector or if AI is used in the response).
Archive & Audit: Maintain a permanent archive of all public statements, social media posts, and media interactions.
Strict Governance: All public communications pre-approved by Legal; all versions and media releases archived per retention policy.
Communication Plans: Implement customer communication plans aligned to business continuity and disaster recovery procedures.
Message Alignment: Ensure consistency between technical restoration activities and customer-facing updates.

Restoration Sign-Off Gate: Implement a mandatory "Technical Sign-Off" protocol where Incident Commanders must validate service stability against pre-defined thresholds before authorizing the "Service Restored" external communication.
Data Validation: Validate all external statements against real-time operational data.
SLA & Legal Review: All customer BC/DR updates reviewed by Legal/Ops; cadence aligned with Service Level Agreements (SLAs).
Outreach Logging: Track and log customer outreach for auditability.
Containment Strategies Network isolation, firewall deny rules, credential/token revocation, API key rotation, or system shutdowns.
Decision Authority: Define roles authorized for containment actions; require risk-based justification for critical system takedowns.
Verification: Validate that containment succeeded and document all actions.
Automation & SOAR: Predefine triggers for automated containment actions (malware isolation, model endpoint disablement).
Evidence Preservation: Capture snapshots, logs, and forensic images before containment modifications when feasible.
Coordination: Communicate containment to impacted business units and track asset restoration readiness.

AI Logic Redirection (Safe Mode): Implement the ability to redirect suspicious or anomalous model traffic to a "sandbox" model or a deterministic rules-based engine during an incident to maintain limited service while containing the primary threat.
Advanced Validation: Automated containment procedures are tested quarterly in live or simulated environments.
Remediation Actions: Patch vulnerabilities, clean malware, rotate credentials, and remove malicious datasets or code.
Validation Tests: Run post-remediation scans, regression tests, and AI inference checks for residual anomalies.
Change Control: Track eradication through formal change records; approvals for returning assets to production.
Documentation: Record eradication steps, validation outcomes, and responsible owners.
Root-Cause Analysis Major Incidents: Identify initial access vector and exploit mechanism; map to control gaps.

Secret & Identity Purging: Execute a comprehensive revocation and re-issuance of all machine identities, API tokens, and service accounts that were active in the compromised environment during the dwell time, regardless of whether they show signs of compromise.
Universal RCA & Validation: Root-cause documented for all incidents; eradication validation performed as a mandatory close-out step.
Restore Procedures: Maintain tested restore runbooks for systems, models, and datasets; verify signatures before redeployment.
Integrity Validation: Validate restored data against checksums; confirm model reproducibility and TEVV compliance.
Verification: Conduct functional and performance validation before public access restoration.
Failover Procedures: Activate secondary sites or alternate clouds per predefined RTO/RPO.
Drills & Maintenance: Annual DR drills; integrity validation after all restorations.

Post-Restore Hardening: Implement a mandatory "patch and harden" step within the recovery workflow to ensure that known vulnerabilities present in the backup image are remediated before the system is reconnected to the production network.
Continuity Plans: Define workarounds, manual fallbacks, communication channels, and minimum operating capacities.
Operational Prioritization: Identify essential business functions, staff assignments, and acceptable degradation levels.
Alternate Procedures: Enable manual service delivery or rerouting to unaffected regions/systems.

Critical Dependency Failover: Establish technical or procedural fallbacks for critical external dependencies (e.g., secondary LLM providers, cached inference modes) to ensure continuity if a primary AI vendor suffers an outage.
Performance Tracking: Monitor SLA impact and customer communication effectiveness during response.
Testing & Validation: Documented continuity plans for all operations; semi-annual testing of fallbacks.
Roles & Responsibilities: Assign roles for detection, triage, ethics escalation, privacy/legal review, and technical containment.
Initial Assessment: Define harm thresholds (ethical, reputational, regulatory). Perform rapid validation using logs, telemetry, and replication tests.
Containment & Recovery: Disable endpoints, roll back models, or revoke model access tokens. Document and isolate affected artifacts and datasets.
AI-Specific Playbooks: Maintain playbooks for prompt injection, leakage, harmful content, bias, and poisoning.
Root Cause & Post-Remediation: Perform model lineage analysis and update training datasets, guardrails, or adversarial detection models. (Corrective & Preventive Action (CAPA))
Communications & Notifications: Notify Ethics/Privacy Committees, CAIO, and impacted customers or regulators when required.
Evidence Preservation: Capture model logs, prompts, outputs, inference data, and relevant metadata for investigation and TEVV validation.
Upstream/Third-Party Reporting: Establish protocols for reporting security and safety failures to third-party model providers or open-source maintainers to facilitate upstream patches.
Lessons Learned: Integrate findings into model risk management, training datasets, and detection rules.
Advanced Oversight: Ethics and Privacy Committee included for all AI & Privacy incidents.
Incident Classification & Severity Matrix: Define categories (intrusion, malware, exfiltration, DDoS, insider misuse). Assign SLAs for triage and containment.
Response Coordination: SOC manages technical actions; communication routed to IR leadership and legal counsel for potential notification.
Forensics & Evidence: Collect and preserve volatile data; maintain chain-of-custody for potential legal review.
Recovery: Eradicate root causes, validate system integrity, patch vulnerabilities, and restore from golden images.
Reporting & Review: Record incident details, actions, and verification outcomes; link to POA&M updates and root-cause records.

Out-of-Band Communication: Establish and maintain a secure, secondary communication platform (e.g., encrypted mobile mesh or isolated SaaS) for IR team coordination when primary corporate networks are compromised.
SOAR Integration: Automate enrichment and containment workflows (host isolation, account disablement, IP blocking).
Quarterly Drills: SLAs and playbooks are tested quarterly in live or tabletop drills.
Privacy Incident Identification: Detect anomalies indicating PII/PHI leakage, misclassification, or unauthorized access.
Notification & Reporting: File regulator notices within statutory deadlines (e.g., 72h); notify affected subjects. DPO oversight is mandatory.
Containment & Eradication: Disable offending processes, revoke tokens, patch misconfigurations, or isolate affected systems.
Documentation & Audit Trail: Maintain privacy incident register; record assessment, actions, and communications.
Assessment & Escalation: Assess likelihood/severity of harm; escalate to Data Protection Officer and Legal for notification assessment.
Post-Breach Improvement: Update controls, training, and contracts to prevent recurrence.

DSR Breach Prioritization: Establish a fast-track process for processing "Right to Erasure" or "Right to Restriction" requests specifically for data subjects affected by a confirmed breach to minimize the duration of risk to their personal data.
Critical Incident PIRs: Conduct Post-Incident Reviews (PIRs) for all critical incidents within 5 business days. Include timeline, decisions, and outcomes.
Root Cause Analysis (RCA): Document systemic, procedural, or human contributors; prioritize recurring control gaps (using 5 Whys or Fishbone).
Action Plan Tracking: Assign remediation tasks with owners and deadlines; monitor progress until validated completion.

Post-Mortem Policy: Enforce a "Blameless" policy for RCAs that focuses on systemic incentives and process failures rather than individual blame, ensuring staff feel safe to report the true root cause.
Integration to Risk & Training: Feed lessons into the risk register, policy updates, runbooks, and awareness training.
Universal Coverage: Post-Incident Reviews (PIRs) conducted for all incidents (minor and major); documented evidence of implemented lessons.
